name: Pusit Relleno
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove squid heads and ink sacs from the squids and clean them both inside and out.
2. Sprinkle salt lightly on the cleaned squids and set aside.
3. In a large bowl, combine ground pork, finely sliced green onions, beaten egg, a small amount of salt, pepper, minced garlic, and vegetable oil.
4. Stuff the squid cavities with the meat mixture. Be careful as it is messy and slippery; avoid overstuffing the calamari, as the filling will expand during cooking. A pastry bag can help with this process.
5. Place a pan in a steamer and steam the stuffed squids covered for 10-15 minutes, or until the filling is cooked through. Drain any excess liquid and set aside.
6. While steaming the calamari, prepare the sauce by heating oil in a skillet over medium heat. Sauté minced garlic until lightly browned, then add chopped onion and stir for one minute.
7. Add chopped tomato, bay leaf, and soy sauce to the skillet, cooking for 3-5 minutes until the tomatoes are soft.
8. Pour the prepared sauce over the stuffed calamari and serve with rice.
Note: The tentacles from the squids can be saved and used as a side dish by stir-frying them with shrimp and vegetables. The broth from steaming the calamari can also be used as a liquid in this dish. This recipe is not suitable for those who dislike squid or are squeamish about its texture.
